content.full_text:
A-77)-[illegible] [illegible] 305

September 21, 1977


James Buchert, Treasurer
Los Padres County Committee
Post Office Box 35
Santa Maria, California  93454

Dear Mr. Buchert:

This is in response to your letter dated July 19, 1977,
concerning information contained in paragraph G on page seven
of our June 1977 "Information Manual on Campaign Disclosure
Provisions of the Political Reform Act." I apologize for the
delay in responding to your letter.

As you may know, Government Code Section 84202 was amended
in 1976, by Charter 1106, to read in pertinent part:

     Committees supporting or opposing the qualification
     of a measure and proponents of a state measure shall
     file campaign statements not later than 35 days after
     the deadline for filing petitions or the date of
     notification that the measure has either qualified or
     failed to qualify, whichever date is earlier . . .

Prior to that amendment, only proponents of measures were
required to file campaign statements which were due 65 days
after either notification of qualification, if the measure qual-
ified, or the deadline for filing petitions, if the measure did
not qualify. Government Code Section 84202 now provides that
committees supporting or opposing the qualification of a measure
are required to file campaign statements. The time for filing
those campaign statements was shortened by the amendment from
65 to 35 days after the deadline for filing petitions or the
notification of qualification or failure to qualify, whichever
was earlier.

There was a reason for having the filing deadline for
campaign statements to be filed under Government Code Section
84202 to be 35 days after the earlier of the two events. As
you are aware, deadlines are established for filing petitions,
such as the one stated in Government Code Section 23324 to

which you referred. Therefore, a campaign statement could be due
35 days after the deadline for filing petitions. However if a
petition is submitted and notification of
failure to qualify is received prior to the filing deadline, the
campaign statement would be due 35 days after the date of notifi-
cation and not 35 days after the deadline for filing petitions.

Paragraph G on page seven of our June 1977 manual explains
the filing procedure required by Government Code Section 84202,
as amended.

I hope this letter clarifies the filing requirements under
Government Code Section 84202. If you have any additional ques-
tions, please feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,


Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

RSK:bw

July 19, 1977


Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Sacramento, Ca 95814

Gentlemen:

     The following quotation is from your Information Manual on Campaign
Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform Act, June, 1977 Revision,
Page 7, Paragraph "G." "All committees supporting or opposing a cir-
culation drive are now required to file campaign statements 35 days after
the measure has qualified for the ballot or 35 days after the final day
for gathering signatures, whichever date is earlier."

     The phrase "or 35 days after the final day for gathering signatures,
whichever date is earlier," appears to be either superfluous or substan-
ially lacking in clarity when examined in the light of government code
sections 23324 and 23326 which respectively spell out a 60 day period for
the accumulatsof signatures upon petitions plus a 30 day period for
the examination of petition signatures.

     The January, 1976 publication of the same manual, Page 5, Paragraph
"F" outlines a filing procedure which does not create the same conflicts.

     In view of the number of petition drives now in progress, this
apparent conflict could result in significant administrative and legal
problems.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        James M. Buchert
                                        Treasurer
                                        Los Padres County Committee
                                        P.O. Box 85
                                        Santa Maria, Ca 93454

JMB:jp
